comparison.new_feed_version: new_IDFM_GTFS_Google_maps_20260611 – Google LLC, pipeline frpridfm, exported 20260610
comparison.important_caveat: These are two DIFFERENT feed products for the same network on the same date — an IDFM-native export vs. a Google-Maps-formatted re-export — not two sequential versions of one feed. Many 'changes' below are export-format differences, not real schedule changes. Internal referential integrity of the new feed is fully intact.

breaking_changes.detail: Three files present in the old feed are absent in the new one. pathways.txt (636 KB of in-station walking links) has no functional replacement; booking_rules.txt and object_codes_extension.txt are IDFM extensions. The new feed adds feed_info.txt, frequencies.txt, id_mappings.txt, and platform_groups.txt instead.
breaking_changes.note: pathways removal is the genuine functional loss — accessibility/in-station routing data is gone. The other two are non-standard extensions.

breaking_changes.detail: 135 routes had route_type remapped from basic to GTFS Extended Route Types (e.g. 3 'Bus' → 702 'Bus Service'; new values 108, 109, 200, 702, 715 appear). Old domain {0,1,2,3,6,7} → new {0,1,3,6,7,108,109,200,702,715}. Value 2 (rail) disappeared from the basic set, folded into extended codes.
breaking_changes.note: A consumer expecting standard route_type values (0–7, 11, 12) will fail to classify ~135 routes. This is the most consequential breaking change for downstream apps.

breaking_changes.detail: pickup_type domain shrank {0,1,2}→{0,1} (value 2 'phone agency' gone); drop_off_type changed {0,1,2}→{0,1,3} (2 removed, 3 'coordinate with driver' added).
breaking_changes.note: Tied to the dropped booking_rules.txt — the flex/on-demand semantics were re-expressed.

breaking_changes.detail: Old uses IDs like 'IDFM:monomodalStopPlace:473875'; new mixes in bare numeric platform IDs like '1371143468'. Stop count 53,979 → 40,772 with 38,781 IDs in common (15,198 old IDs absent). The new id_mappings.txt file exists specifically to bridge old↔new IDs.
breaking_changes.note: NOT internally breaking — all 10.38M new stop_times rows resolve against new stops (0 missing FKs). The id_mappings.txt is the publisher's explicit acknowledgment that IDs were remapped; external systems holding old stop IDs should use it to translate.

suspicious_changes.detail: calendar services 989 → 9,780 (+889%); calendar_dates rows 2,067 → 27,009 (+1,207%); service_id overlap is 0 (entirely regenerated). exception_type domain {1,2}→{1} (no more 'service removed' exceptions).
suspicious_changes.note: Different service-modeling strategy between the two exporters — Google explodes services into many granular service_ids. All trips' service_ids resolve, so it's consistent, but it's a total calendar reorganization.

suspicious_changes.detail: Stop count dropped 53,979 → 40,772 (-24.5%), exceeding the 20% threshold.
suspicious_changes.note: Largely the entrance/quay nodes that move into platform_groups.txt under the Google format, not real stop removals.

suspicious_changes.detail: Envelope contracted: old [47.96108, 1.14986]–[49.45933, 3.56067] → new [48.0067, 1.3474]–[49.2855, 3.56067]. The min-lat and max-lat edges moved ~5–18 km inward.
suspicious_changes.note: Consistent with -24.5% fewer stops trimming a few outlying nodes; not a coordinate-system error (common stops are co-located).

suspicious_changes.detail: Old feed had no feed_info.txt; new feed declares feed_publisher_name 'Google LLC' (vs. the network operator IDFM). Publisher URL points to Google Maps transit.
suspicious_changes.note: Expected given this is the Google-formatted product, but a publisher swap is exactly the kind of source-change the rule is meant to catch.

suspicious_changes.detail: Unique shape_ids 10,089 → 10,744, but 0 in common — every shape_id was regenerated under the new scheme. New shapes add shape_dist_traveled and shape_pt_source_google columns.
suspicious_changes.note: Geometry continuity unverifiable due to total ID turnover; all trips.shape_id resolve against new shapes (0 missing).

checks_passed.removed_required_field: none — routes.txt dropped route_desc/route_sort_order (optional); stops.txt dropped stop_timezone (optional); stop_times.txt dropped the flex-window and booking-rule fields (optional, tied to removed booking_rules.txt)
checks_passed.changed_field_type_or_format: none; all 10,380,141 new stop_times rows have valid HH:MM:SS times (empty times handled as flex, none malformed). Note: new feed quotes all CSV fields, old does not — cosmetic only
checks_passed.referential_integrity_new_feed: clean — stop_times→stops 0 missing, stop_times→trips 0 missing, trips→routes/service/shapes 0 missing, stops→parent_station 0 missing
checks_passed.feed_validity_window: new feed valid 2026-06-05 → 2026-07-09 (in the future); old calendar spanned 2026-06-08 → 2026-07-10. Slight start-date shift earlier, no coverage gap
checks_passed.entity_count_deltas_within_threshold: trips -0.2% (470,037→468,947, 447,255 common), stop_times -0.4%, routes -2.8% (56 removed, 0 added)
checks_passed.stops_moved_over_500m: 2 of 38,781 common stops — 'Les Chênes' (612 m) and 'Gare Montparnasse' (500.4 m, right at threshold). Both minor recentering, not coordinate errors
checks_passed.stop_time_ordering: 0 monotonicity violations across all 10.38M timed rows
checks_passed.headsign_route_name_bulk_change: 1.2% of common route_long_names changed — below bulk threshold
